office: House Committee on Veterans' Affairs (Democratic Staff)
description:
The Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs seeks full or part-time legislative or press interns. Responsibilities include but are not limited to conducting legislative research, answering phones, attending briefings and hearings for staff, compiling press clips, drafting content, and other tasks depending on the needs of the Committee and the intern's interests.

Qualified candidates will have strong oral and written communications skills as well as a desire to help veterans and learn about the House and legislative process.
responsibilities: Attending briefings and hearings for staff
Performing other tasks depending on the needs of the Committee and the intern's interests
qualifications: Strong oral and written communications skills
Desire to help veterans and learn about the House and legislative process
how_to_apply: To apply, fill out the online form at https://veterans.house.gov/contact/internship and submit a resume and cover letter to veterans.internships@mail.house.gov. Please include in your cover letter how many days a week you would be available to intern as well as your preferred start and end dates. Indicate in the subject line whether you are applying for a legislative or press internship.

position_title: Labor Policy Intern Or Labor Policy Fellow
office: Committee On Education And Labor, Majority
description:
The Committee on Education and Labor, Majority is currently seeking a second or third year graduate or law student as a 'Labor Policy Intern' or 'Labor Policy Fellow' for the spring of 2020. Interns and Fellows perform a variety of tasks that support the staff in our legislative work.

Applicants should be able to start immediately and commit through May. Graduates and Fellows should be able to demonstrate study, practical or policy experience in issues relevant to the Committee. Fellows must be participating in an established fellowship program.
responsibilities: Conduct legislative research, legal research, and fact checking
Perform technical writing including legislation, memos, and speeches
Assist staff in hearing and markup preparation and execution
Attend hearings, markups, briefings and meetings for the purpose of informing staff and Members
qualifications: Second or third year graduate or law student
Ability to start immediately and commit through May
Demonstrated study, practical or policy experience in issues relevant to the Committee
Participation in an established fellowship program (for Fellows)
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email their cover letter, resume and internship applicant information form to E&L_Interns&Fellows@mail.house.gov. Please, also, include two short, relevant writing samples in your application materials. Applicants may visit our website at https://edlabor.house.gov/about/internships for more information.
